channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Mamdani says New York City can't arrest Netanyahu but the federal government should
item.description: New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.
item.content:encoded:
New York Mayor Mamdani said the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.

(Image credit: Anna Connors/Anna Connors)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: French lawmakers approve a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
item.description: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.
item.content:encoded:
The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.

(Image credit: Nicolas Garriga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Staying in a job for the health insurance? About 1 in 4 Americans do, a survey says
item.description: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: Creative Images Lab)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: House votes to extend government funding, aiming to avoid an election year shutdown
item.description: Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last September. The stopgap funding measure now heads to the Senate, where it faces an uncertain future.
item.content:encoded:
Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last September. The stopgap funding measure now heads to the Senate, where it faces an uncertain future.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Smithsonian too focused on 'oppressors and the oppressed,' say House Republicans
item.description: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.
item.content:encoded:
In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
item.description: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

(Image credit: Alfredo Zuniga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Is that food really made with 100% avocado oil? Likely not
item.description: A new study finds that consumers who are paying a premium for foods advertised as made with avocado oil are probably not getting what they think they're buying.
item.content:encoded:
A new study finds that consumers who are paying a premium for foods advertised as made with avocado oil are probably not getting what they think they're buying.

(Image credit: fcafotodigital)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: The best games of 2026 so far, picked by NPR's staff
item.description: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.content:encoded: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
item.description: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.
item.content:encoded:
The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

(Image credit: Shekhar Yadav)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
